stories.headline: Gates Foundation commits $200M to Anthropic for global health AI work
stories.blurb: The Bill & Melinda Gates Foundation is investing $200 million in Anthropic to develop AI tools for global health and development challenges. The partnership will focus on building Claude-based solutions for disease surveillance, agricultural planning, and educational access in low-resource settings.
stories.why_it_matters: One of the world's largest philanthropies is now directly funding frontier AI development for public health.
stories.original_headline: Anthropic forms $200 million partnership with the Gates Foundation

stories.headline: Nvidia's CEO says China will let US AI chips back in
stories.blurb: Jensen Huang, speaking days after joining Trump's China summit, said he expects Chinese authorities to eventually allow American AI chip imports again. No timeline was given, but it signals a potential thaw in tech trade restrictions that have shaped the chip industry for years.
stories.why_it_matters: If it happens, US chipmakers could access the world's largest AI market again.
stories.original_headline: China Will Open Its Market to AI Chips From US, Nvidia’s CEO Says

try_this_prompt.title: The confidence audit
try_this_prompt.prompt: I'm about to paste something I wrote that sounds confident. Your job: find every sentence where I'm actually guessing, hedging, or hoping no one asks a follow-up question. Quote each one back to me, then tell me which gaps I can fill in an hour and which ones mean I'm not ready to send this yet.
try_this_prompt.tool_hint: Before you hit send on anything that commits your team to a timeline or a number.

daily_question: If you could pick one global problem for AI companies to fund next, what would it be?
notes: Engineer slot lacks viable candidates: top candidate (id=44 Mexico breach) is security-incident framing that fails excitement test; remaining candidates are either stale (April/Feb dates: Tiny Aya Feb 17, Muse Spark Apr 8, DiLoCo Apr 23, co-clinician Apr 30), workforce sociology (Meta 7K workers = labor market macro), or research papers without product hooks. Scout gaps confirmed: no mobile/wearable products, no pricing/access news, no coding-agent updates in current pool. Adjustments: Shipping 2 stories: (1) Anthropic/Gates partnership - clean business power-move; (2) China AI chip market opening - substantive infrastructure/market story. Both current (May 14-18), both Tier 1, different vendors, different angles (partnership vs market/trade).
